today.title: Today
today.restDescription: Today is rest day. Recommended: stretching, walking, sleep, and recovery activities.
today.confirmCancelWorkout: Cancel workout? All unsaved data will be lost.
today.resetWorkoutConfirm: Reset the last workout? All set completion data will be deleted.

recovery.title: Recovery Score
recovery.description: Comprehensive recovery index (0–100%) based on HRV, sleep, resting HR, and subjective feelings. Higher = better recovery.

log.insufficientDataChart: 📊 Recovery Score vs. Sleep chart will appear after 7+ days of data. It will show how sleep quality affects recovery.
log.noDataHeatmap: 🗓️ Weekly heatmap will display your metrics by day after 7+ check-ins. It will help you see trends.
log.insufficientDataCorrelations: Correlations between your habits and Recovery Score will appear here when enough data accumulates. Keep filling in your check-ins.

analytics.needMinimumCheckins: Need at least 2 check-ins. Complete today and tomorrow — first charts will appear.
analytics.title: Analytics

weekReview.nextWeekMaintain: Volume unchanged — one more good week and we go up

exercise.apreTooltip: Auto-regulatory load: weight adjusts to your current state. Set 3 — AMRAP (max reps), Set 4 weight adjusts based on results.
exercise.unconfigured: Exercise not configured. Set initial parameters for APRE calculation.

recoveryBanner.red: ⛔ Active recovery day. Weight reduced by {{reduction}} {{unit}} to protect CNS.
recoveryBanner.yellow: ⚠️ Recovery reduced. Lower intensity to RPE 6–7, avoid failure.

exercise.calisthenicsWeightDescription: How many reps can you do with added weight?
exercise.calisthenicsWeightExample: Example: 6 pull-ups with +5 kg backpack → enter weight 5, reps 6

profile.title: Profile

rehab.title: Rehab
rehab.description: Daily exercises from your training plan to correct problem areas.
rehab.subtitle: Dosing and frequency follow the clinical protocol.
rehab.exercisesHint: Enter exercise IDs separated by commas (e.g: squat, deadlift)

info.title: 📖 Guide
info.description: Heart rate zones (Z1–Z5), HRV guide and status explanations — what each readiness color means and how to act.

methodology.title: Methodology
methodology.description: Open formulas: how Recovery Score is calculated, why load changes, and where weight coefficients come from.
methodology.subtitle: Scientific base: APRE (meta-analysis 2021), Oura Readiness, Firstbeat methodology.

nutrition.title: 🍽️ Nutrition
nutrition.description: Personal macros and supplement plan with rationale for current training block.
nutrition.subtitle: Advice changes based on day (training/rest), Recovery Score, and muscle soreness.

exercises.title: 🏋️ Exercises
exercises.description: Configure starting weights and APRE protocols for all exercises.

data.title: 💾 Data
data.resetWarning: Delete all data? This action cannot be undone.
data.noBackups: No backups. They are created automatically before data reset.
data.restoreWarning: Restoring will replace all current data. Continue?

profile.confirmDeleteDesc: Delete all data? This action cannot be undone.
profile.confirmResetDesc: Reset all exercise settings? This action cannot be undone.

integration.emailHint: Leave your email to be notified when ready

welcome.title: Welcome!
welcome.description: Smart Fitness Coach — a personal trainer that adapts load to your condition.
features: 🔒 All data on device, no cloud

trainingDays.title: Choose Training Days
trainingDays.description: We recommend 3 days per week with rest between workouts. Order: A → B → C.

z1.name: Warmup
z1.desc: Nose breathing, talking without effort. No strain at all.

z2.name: Aerobic Base
z2.desc: Full sentences. Can sing — with difficulty. A bit warm.
z2.use: MAIN zone. 80% of all runs. VO2max grows here

z3.name: Tempo
z3.desc: Short phrases, not sentences. Noticeable breathing.
z3.use: M2–M3 only. Short inserts ≤10 min. If chest tight — drop down

z4.name: Threshold
z4.desc: ⚠ Words only. Loud breathing. Cannot sustain long.
z4.use: Intervals ≤90 sec in M3 only. Bronchospasm risk with prolonged work

z5.name: Maximum
z5.desc: 🚫 Cannot talk at all. Cannot sustain >30–60 sec.
z5.use: Excluded initially and for athletes with asthma. Introduced in M3+ only.

toast.importError: Could not import file. Check JSON format.

recoveryScore.title: Recovery Score
recoveryScore.description: Comprehensive recovery index (0–100%). Combines HRV, sleep, resting HR, and subjective feelings. Green = ready for full load, yellow = caution, red = rest.

readiness.title: Readiness
readiness.description: Final training status based on Recovery Score and check-in data.

hrv.title: HRV (Heart Rate Variability)
hrv.description: Measures nervous system balance. Higher HRV usually indicates good recovery and readiness for training.

apre.title: APRE (Autoregulatory Progressive Resistance Exercise)
apre.description: Scientific method for automatic weight adjustment. Weight changes based on reps performed in set 3 (AMRAP).

rpe.title: RPE (Rating of Perceived Exertion)
rpe.description: Subjective difficulty rating on scale 0–10. Helps track progression and avoid overtraining.

deload.title: Deload Week
deload.description: Every 4th week volume drops by 40%. Allows nervous system recovery and preparation for new cycle.

sleep.title: Sleep
sleep.description: Sleep quantity and quality are key recovery factors. 7–9 hours recommended for active people.

restingHR.title: Resting HR
restingHR.description: Heart rate immediately after waking. Elevated resting HR may signal poor recovery.

exercise.title: APRE Exercise
exercise.description: APRE exercises have 4 sets: 2 warmup sets, AMRAP (max reps), and adjustment set.

sparkline.title: 7-Day Trend
sparkline.description: Mini-chart showing metric dynamics over the past week. Tap for detailed view.

streak.title: Day Streak
streak.description: Consecutive days with check-ins. Maintain consistency for better progress!

step1.title: Recovery Score — Your Readiness Indicator
step1.content: This ring shows how well you've recovered. Green = full training, yellow = reduce intensity, red = rest day. Tap the ring to see detailed metrics.

step2.title: 7-Day Trends
step2.content: These mini-charts show your key metrics: HRV (heart rate variability), sleep, and load (RPE). Trends help visualize overall recovery picture.

step3.title: Today's Training Plan
step3.content: Shows workout type (A, B, or C) and exercise list. On rest days, you'll see recovery recommendations.

step4.title: APRE — Smart Weight Progression
step4.content: APRE exercises automatically adjust weight for next week. Do max reps in set 3 (AMRAP), and the system calculates new weight.

step5.title: Rate Your Workout (RPE)
step5.content: After training, rate difficulty on scale 0–10. Helps track progression and avoid overtraining. 7–8 is good working intensity.

step6.title: Quick Actions
step6.content: Mark morning activation and evening recovery routines. These help maintain fitness and reduce injury risk.

step7.title: What's Next Tomorrow
step7.content: Preview of next day's plan helps mentally prepare. If tomorrow is rest — plan other activities.

step8.title: Navigation & Profile
step8.content: Use bottom menu to switch sections: Log (history), Analytics (trends), Profile (settings). Happy training!
